Great influencers educate others into a sin without any threats. Because the principles of persuasion are all anchored in published science, they're more trustworthy than other advice you may have encountered. But these principles are deceptively simple at the same time. They're so simple that many people shrug and say, this is all common sense, nothing for me here to think too deeply about or plan my work around. But actually, there's much more to them, especially when it comes time to putting the principles into practice. And often, the science proves something different than common sense or folk wisdom would lead you to believe. Which is good, because our initial assumptions and folk wisdom are often incorrect. Thankfully, there are decades of scientific research to draw on for better knowledge and practice. In the past, persuasion was thought of as only an art. But now that there is a science on the topic, we know that persuasion can be taught and learned. And the research is clear that people who learn to apply these teachings to their real world environment can become as effective as those who have a natural talent for being persuasive. This training will teach you how communicators fall short when they don't understand how to apply these scientifically proven techniques for influence.
